title: Gingerly Grilled Malaysian Lemongrass Satay
description: Peppery, aerated, festive Malaysian dish featuring lemongrass, perfect for outdoor gatherings. Diabetic-friendly friendly due to minimal added sugar.

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Reduce amount if you're not used to fish sauce.

instructions: Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes before grilling.
In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, fish sauce, palm sugar, and 2 cloves of crushed ginger. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine chicken breast, minced cilantro, crushed ginger, and salt. Toss to coat.
Add the blended coconut milk mixture to the chicken mixture and mix well.
Thread chicken onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the satay on a raclette grill or a charcoal grill preheated to medium-high heat (about 180°C/350°F).
Brush the satay with sesame oil and cook for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
When serving, garnish with lemongrass stalks and serve with fresh lime wedges on the side.
Serve immediately, as the satay can become dry if left to sit for too long.
